DATuM
  • preprocessor.py
  • View page source

preprocessor.py

Provides routines for pre-processing the BeVERLI Hill stereo PIV data.

datum.preprocessor.preprocess_data(piv_obj)

Comprehensively pre-processes the BeVERLI Hill stereo PIV data.

Parameters:

piv_obj (Piv) – Object containing the BeVERLI Hill stereo PIV data.

Return type:

None

datum.preprocessor.get_coordinate_transformation_parameters(piv_obj)

Obtains the coordinate transformation parameters for the BeVERLI Hill stereo PIV data.

Parameters:

piv_obj (Piv) – Object containing the BeVERLI Hill stereo PIV data.

Return type:

None

datum.preprocessor.transform_data(piv_obj)

Performs the Cartesian coordinate transformation from the local stereo PIV coordinate system to the global Cartesian coordinate system fo the BeVERLI experiment in the Virginia Tech Stability Wind Tunnel.

Parameters:

piv_obj (Piv) – Object containing the BeVERLI Hill stereo PIV data.

Return type:

None

datum.preprocessor.transform_data_without_interpolation(piv_obj)

Performs the Cartesian coordinate transformation from the local stereo PIV coordinate system to the global Cartesian coordinate system fo the BeVERLI experiment in the Virginia Tech Stability Wind Tunnel.

Parameters:

piv_obj (Piv) – Object containing the BeVERLI Hill stereo PIV data.

Return type:

None

datum.preprocessor.compute_velocity_gradient(piv_obj)

Computes the mean velocity gradient tensor from the BeVERLI Hill stereo PIV mean velocity data.

Parameters:

piv_obj (Piv) – Object containing the BeVERLI Hill stereo PIV data.

Return type:

None

datum.preprocessor.get_computable_velocity_gradient_components(piv_obj)

Calculates the directly computable components of the mean velocity gradient tensor from the BeVERLI Hill stereo PIV mean velocity data.

Parameters:

piv_obj (Piv) – Object containing the BeVERLI Hill stereo PIV data.

Return type:

Dict[str, ndarray]

Returns:

A dictionary containing NumPy ndarrays of shape (m, n), where m and n represent the number of available data points in the x:sub:1- and x:sub:2-direction. Each array represents a computable component of the mean velocity gradient tensor

datum.preprocessor.get_strain_and_rotation_tensor(piv_obj)

Obtains the mean rate-of-strain and rotation tensors.

Parameters:

piv_obj (Piv) – Object containing the BeVERLI Hill stereo PIV data.

Return type:

None

datum.preprocessor.get_eddy_viscosity(piv_obj)

Obtains the eddy viscosity.

Parameters:

piv_obj (Piv) – Object containing the BeVERLI Hill stereo PIV data.

Return type:

None

datum.preprocessor.get_preprocessed_data()

Attempts to load pre-processed BeVERLI Hill stereo PIV data if the user runs the pre-processor with the deactivated flag.

Return type:

datum.my_types.PivData

Returns:

The pre-processed BeVERLI Hill stereo PIV data.
